claims.text: Patient 2 carried c.1935C>A (p.Asp645Glu) in exon 14 (catalytic GH31 domain) inherited from father, compound heterozygous with c.1933G>C (p.Asp645His) inherited from mother, with undetectable GAA activity (0% of normal).

citations.quote:
2 | 0 | c.1935C>A | 14 | p.Asp645Glu | Catalytic GH31 | FA | Hermans et al. [13]
| | | c.1933G>C | 14 | p.Asp645His | Catalytic GH31 | MO | Lin&Shieh [15]

claims.text: Patient 2, carrying p.Asp645Glu compound heterozygous with p.Asp645His, had onset at 1–2 months, was diagnosed at 1–2 months with tachypnea and mild hepatomegaly, received ERT since 4 weeks of age, and had a favorable outcome at 4 years.

citations.quote: 2 | 1-2 m | 1-2 m | tachypnea, mild hepatomegaly | short PR interval, BVH, EF 30% | yes, since 4 wk./ favorable outcome at 4 y | no, but two previous deceased children with cardiomyopathy

claims.text: The p.Asp645Glu mutation (c.1935C>A) is described as associated with IOPD of Taiwanese descendants and as a founder mutation among Chinese and Taiwanese IOPD patients.

citations.quote: p.Asp645Glu associated with IOPD of Taiwanese descendants [14]
the p.Asp645Glu is more common among Chinese and Taiwanese IOPD patients, and as a founder mutation [14]

claims.text: The prevalence of p.Asp645Glu/His among Thai patients with IOPD (including data from this study) was refined to 32.4% (11/34 alleles), with exon 14 being the hotspot for GAA mutation at 50% (17/34 alleles).

citations.quote: Including the data from the present study, the prevalence of p.Asp645Glu/His is refined to 32.4% (11/34) and exon 14 was the hot spot for GAA mutation at 50% (17/34)

claims.text: Previous in vitro expression studies confirmed significant loss of GAA activity for p.Asp645Glu, and it has been shown to cause delayed intracellular transport, deficient phosphorylation, and proteolytic processing.

citations.quote: Previous in vitro expression studies of the mutations p.Gly293Arg, p.Trp367Arg, p.Asp645Glu, and p.Cys647Trp have confirmed significant loss of GAA activity and these variants were found in IOPD patients [23, 31–33]. The p.Asp645Glu variant has been shown to cause delayed intracellular transport, deficient phosphorylation and proteolytic processing [25].

claims.text: The p.Asp645Glu variant is referenced in this paper as previously reported by Hermans et al. and is not a novel mutation; it is listed with a ClinVar submission in Table 3 context alongside other variants.

citations.quote: c.1935C>A | 14 | p.Asp645Glu | Catalytic GH31 | FA | Hermans et al. [13]

claims.text: Mutation screening of exons 14 and 5 (which harbor p.Asp645Glu) yielding 62% (21/34) detection rate is proposed as a cost-effective strategy for rapid confirmation of IOPD among Thai and Southeast Asian descendants.

citations.quote: Mutation screening of exons 14 and 5, yielding 62% (21/34) detection rate, may serve as a cost-effective strategy for rapid confirmation of IOPD among Thai and Southeast Asian descendants.
